{"step_by_step_thinking": "Parkinsonism is associated with tremors, often described as pill-rolling tremor, which can involve the tongue. Alcohol abuse can lead to coarse tremors of the tongue as a result of cerebellar degeneration. Thyrotoxicosis, or hyperthyroidism, can cause coarse tremors, including in the tongue. General paresis, a form of late-stage neurosyphilis, can present with coarse intention tremors of the tongue. Therefore, coarse tremors of the tongue are seen in all except Parkinsonism, which primarily presents with resting or action tremors rather than coarse intention tremors.", "answer_choice": "A"}
